observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal
Accountant:
a) Ensure that the Road Networks account is recognized only upon the
completion and turnover of the project, and that depreciation
commences only from that date, in adherence to the Revised Chart of
Accounts for LGUs under COA Circular No. 2015-009 and Paragraph 71
of IPSAS 17; and
b) Prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the overstated
depreciation due to erroneous computation for fair presentation in the
financial statements, as follows:
Accumulated Depreciation-Road Networks  (133,526.97)
Depreciation - Infrastructure Assets  (19,075.28)
Prior Period Adjustment  (114,451.69)

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal
Accountant prepare adjusting entries to restore to the Cash in Bank account
the unreleased checks and record the appropriate payable or liability
accounts for fair presentation of financial statements as at yearend and
reverse the same in the ensuing year.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to adhere the
computation of depreciation of PPEs provided in Paragraph 71 of IPSAS 17
and Section 04 (o) of MNGAS for LGUs; review the depreciation schedule;
and prepare the necessary adjustments to correct the depreciation expense
and the carrying value of the PPEs for fair presentation in the financial
statements.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the
necessary adjusting entries to correct the erroneous recognition of the
discounts on RPT/SET collected in advance in adherence to the RCA for
LGUs under COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommended and the Management agreed that:
a. The Municipal Accountant and Acting Municipal Treasurer reconcile
their records to determine the correct account balances relative to the
collection of RPT;
b. The Municipal Accountant prepare necessary adjusting journal entries
based on the result of the reconciliation; and
c. The Acting Municipal Treasurer completely fill-out the Report of
Collections and Deposits for prior, current, and advance collections, and
the corresponding discounts and penalties including the provincial and
barangay shares for check and balance.

We recommended and the Management agreed that:
a) The Municipal Accountant prepare a subsidiary ledger for each
barangay showing the RPT shares due to them within a given period to
determine the current outstanding amounts and to ensure accurate and
timely distribution of RPT shares; and
b) The Acting Municipal Treasurer implement the necessary actions to
facilitate the immediate release of the RPT shares to the barangays in
compliance with Section 271(d) of RA No. 7160 to fund the
implementation of development programs, projects, and activities.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a) Inform the concerned DILG Office on any changes in the DILG endorsed
GPB and/or necessity to implement additional PPAs relevant to current
gender issues or GAD related undertakings for determination of its
compliance with JMC No. 2016-01; and
b) Direct the GAD Focal Person to submit the annual GPB and the
Accomplishment Report to the Audit Team within the prescribed period
pursuant to Item V of COA Circular 2014-001 for timely evaluation and
relevant action.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the MSWD Head to issue
a guarantee letter to the service provider for assistance above
 (10,000.00) pursuant with DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 6 series of 2023.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the department/section
heads ensure that job order personnel prepare and submit their actual
accomplishment reports to support services rendered pursuant to
Section 1.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Acting
Municipal Treasurer:
a) Closely coordinate with the BLGF to establish clear-cut achievable
revenue collection targets;
b) Conduct a comprehensive and periodic review of property ownership
records, including coordination with Barangays and other offices to trace
delinquent taxpayers, to improve taxpayer data that will increase the
success rate in serving notices and enforcing collections; and
c) Explore and implement additional measures to strengthen tax collection
initiatives, especially the delinquent accounts, through enhanced tax
information and education campaigns to improve taxpayer compliance;
and establish clear performance targets and robust monitoring
mechanisms to track revenue collection progress, identify gaps, and
implement timely improvements.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the:
a) The Building Official and the Acting Municipal Treasurer comply with the
prescribed procedural guidelines for the disposition and use of the 20
percent share from the income generated from building permit fees and
other charges set forth under DPWH and DILG Joint Memorandum
Circular No. 001 dated July 4, 2013;
b) The Acting Municipal Treasurer determine the 20 percent share of the
income generated from building permit fees and other charges from
CY 2013 to CY 2024 and remit the total amount of the five percent
DPWH share to the DPWH Secretary under Special Account No. 154
thru the Bureau of Treasury;
c) The Acting Municipal Treasurer issue an official receipt indicating the
breakdown for the sharing of income generated from building permit
fees and other charges and open and maintain a Trust Fund Account
for the said purpose; and
d) The Municipal Building Official utilize the 15 percent share of the
Municipality for the operating and incidental expenses related to the
functions of the OBO by preparing a Program of Work detailing the
manner the 15 percent share would be utilized subject to the approval
by the Local Chief Executive.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
The City School Board stop the practice of charging expenses not authorized under Section 272 of the Local Government Code and not among the allowable expenses chargeable against the Fund pursuant to Item 4.0 of Joint Circular No. 1, series of 2017 dated January 19, 2017.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review all disbursements and ascertain that they are properly supported with appropriate and necessary documents before certifying the disbursements pursuant to Section 38.2 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for LGUs, Volume 1.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor adhere to the fundamental principles governing the financial transactions and operations of the government agency provided under Section 4 of PD No. 1445, one of these is that claims against government funds shall be supported with complete documentation.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer direct all accountable officers (collecting or disbursing officer) to prepare the Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF) monthly pursuant to Section 50 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for LGUs, Volume II.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the LDRRM Council propose for the revision of the LDRRM Plan and introduce new activities and projects that would reduce, avoid, respond and manage the adverse impacts of disasters in order to achieve the country’s overall goal of “safer, adaptive and disaster resilient communities towards sustainable development.”

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the DRMM Officer or In Charge of the DRRMO to implement the plan based on timelines and must ensure that DRRMF is utilized for the purpose of supporting disaster risk reduction and management activities.

Recommendation:
We recommended that:
The Municipal Mayor instruct the inventory committee composed of the representative from the Mayor’s Office, Municipal Treasurer’s Office and the Accounting Office to undertake the physical count of inventories and PPEs as soon as possible.
He must require the committee to submit the RPCI/RPCPPE as soon as the conduct of inventory is completed.
It is also recommended that the Accounting and Municipal Treasurer’s Offices undertake a regular reconciliation of their records in order to have a fair presentation of the inventory and PPE accounts in the financial statements.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipality strictly comply with the provisions of Republic Act 9003, especially with the minimum requirements for the operation of a controlled dumpsite pursuant to Section 37 thereof until such time that a landfill is constructed.
We also recommended that the Municipality expedite the construction of landfill so it could comply with the requirements of R.A. 9003 in its ecological solid waste management.
Moreover, we recommended that the Municipality continue to implement source reduction, recycling and composting programs through waste diversion activities pursuant to Section 20 of RA 9003 to reduce the volume of solid wastes.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor and the Municipal Accountant require the accountable officer to submit immediately the Liquidation Reports and supporting documents to settle the unliquidated amount of ₱456,195.96.
Henceforth, all cash advances must be liquidated within the prescribed period and closed at year-end, pursuant to 5.8 of COA Circular 97-002.

Recommendation:
We recommended that:
The Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit immediately the undeposited collections of ₱2,956,113.41 and adhere to the existing regulation to deposit intact the collections daily or not later than the next banking day pursuant to Sec. 32 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume 1.

We recommended that:

The Municipal Mayor create an inventory committee composed of the representative from the Mayor’s Office, Municipal Treasurer’s Office and the Accounting Office to undertake the physical count of inventories and PPEs.

He must immediately direct the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of inventories and PPEs and to submit the RPCI/RPCPPE as soon as the conduct of inventory is completed.  

The Accounting and Municipal Treasurer’s Offices undertake a regular reconciliation of their records in order to have a fair presentation of the inventory and PPE accounts in the financial statement.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant exert effort for the immediate preparation of the BRS so the Municipality and other users of the report will be informed of the status of the bank accounts and in case of errors between the balance per books and the bank, they could be detected at once.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant furnish the Audit Team a copy of the BRS on time so the Team could conduct timely verification of the report.  In order to ensure timely submission of BRS, the Municipal Accountant should make representations with the depository bank to furnish the Municipality, copies of the Bank Statements at the earliest possible time.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer ensure proper charging of expenses to the 20% Development Fund and submit justification for aforesaid payment which was charged to the Fund.

We recommended that the Municipality abide with the existing rules and regulations on the utilization of the Local Risk Reduction Management Fund pursuant to RA 10121 and submit justification why such payment was charged to the said fund.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:

           We recommended that:


           a) The Municipal Mayor create an inventory committee composed of
              the  representative  from  the  Mayor’s   Office,   Municipal
              Treasurer’s Office and the Accounting Office to undertake the
              physical count of inventories and PPEs.


           b) He must immediately direct the Inventory Committee to conduct
              the physical count of inventories and PPEs and to submit  the
              RPCI/RPCPPE as soon as the conduct of inventory is completed.




           c) The Accounting and Municipal Treasurer’s Offices undertake  a
              regular reconciliation of their records in order  to  have  a
              fair presentation of the inventory and PPE  accounts  in  the
              financial statements.

Recommendation:


           We recommended that the Municipal Accountant  exert  effort  for
      the immediate preparation of the BRS so  the  Municipality  and  other
      users of the report will  be  informed  of  the  status  of  the  bank
      accounts and in case of errors between the balance per books  and  the
      bank, they could be detected at once.


           We also recommended that the Municipal  Accountant  furnish  the
      Audit Team a copy of the BRS on time so the Team could conduct  timely
      verification of the report.  In order to ensure timely  submission  of
      BRS, the Municipal Accountant should  make  representations  with  the
      depository bank to  furnish  the  Municipality,  copies  of  the  Bank
      Statements at the earliest possible time.

Recommendation:

           We recommended that the Municipal Development Council abide with
      the guidelines issued by DILG and DBM under JMC No. 2011-1 dated April
      13, 2011 in prioritizing projects for inclusion in the  Municipality’s
      Annual Investment Plan or 20% IRA for developmental projects.


           We also  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Accountant  and  the
      Municipal Budget Officer ensure proper charging of expenses to the 20%
      Development Fund and submit justification for aforesaid payment  which
      was charged to the Fund.

Recommendation:

           We recommended that the Municipality  abide  with  the  existing
      rules and regulations on the utilization of the Local  Risk  Reduction
      Management Fund pursuant to RA 10121 and submit justification why such
      payment was charged to the said fund.

Recommendation

           We recommended that the City Local  School  Board  refrain  from
      charging non-related  expenses  to  the  Special  Education  Fund  and
      strictly adhere to the regulation on the  prioritization  of  expenses
      charged against the School Board Fund as provided  under  Article  363
      (c) of the IRR of RA 7160 and Section 4 of  DECS-DBM-DILG  JC  No.  01
      series of 1998 dated April 14, 1998.

Recommendation:


            We recommended that the  Municipal  Government  strictly  comply
      with Sections 2(d) and 21 of Republic Act 9003,  with  regard  to  the
      monitoring of the implementation of the Solid  Waste  Management  Plan
      and the mandatory segregation of solid wastes, respectively.


           We also reiterated these recommendations made by the  Provincial
      Solid Waste Management Monitoring Team per its report:


           |Reorganize the MSWMB with Technical Working Group to    |
|support the implementation and monitoring of SWM program|
|Conduct regular meeting to discuss updates of SWM       |
|program implementation                                  |
|Coordinate with MGB-DENR for the conduct of site        |
|geological assessment for the preparation of site       |
|development plan for ECO Park if site is feasible       |
|Update and implement plan (IEC Plan, Monitoring and     |
|Enforcement Pl                                          |
|Production and distribution of IEC materials according  |
|to target stakeholders                                  |
|Formulation of MOA with various stakeholders to ensure  |
|sustainability of SWM program like junkshop owners,     |
|women’s group, DepEd family and religious group, etc.   |
|Organize, train and deputize Enforcement Team           |
|Documentation of every activity                         |
|Augment additional workforce to focus on the            |
|implementation of SWM program                           |
|Encourage participation and involvement of all barangays|
|in the implementation of SWM program                    |
